title: Open X-Embodiment / RT-X
why_it_matters: The default starting point for multi-embodiment learning.

title: Octo
embodiments: multiple robot arms and mobile manipulators
why_it_matters: A practical open-source baseline for generalist robot policies.

title: Scaling Cross-Embodied Learning / CrossFormer
why_it_matters: Shows a one-policy framing across broad embodiment families.

title: Being-H0.5
shared_component: shared human-centric VLA backbone + shared motor primitives
why_it_matters: Strongest recent framing for human traces as universal prior.

title: RDT2
embodiments: novel robot platforms via UMI-style data
embodiment_specific: robot-specific deployment through embodiment-agnostic UMI interface
interface: UMI-style action interface with RVQ and flow matching
evaluation: zero-shot generalization to unseen objects scenes instructions and robotic platforms
why_it_matters: Tests how far scaled UMI data can push zero-shot cross-embodiment VLA deployment.

title: GR00T N1
why_it_matters: Industrial evidence that humanoid learning is becoming multi-embodiment.

title: Manipulator-Independent Representations for Visual Imitation
why_it_matters: A foundational paper on transfer via embodiment-agnostic visual representations.

title: XIRL
why_it_matters: A foundational paper for cross-embodiment reward learning.

title: XSkill
why_it_matters: A strong skill-centric representation learning paper.

title: WHIRL
why_it_matters: A key real-world bridge from human video to robot imitation.

title: Structure-Aware Transformer Policy for Inhomogeneous Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning
evaluation: multi-task and transfer learning across different action/state dimensions
why_it_matters: An early transformer-based morphology-aware policy for inhomogeneous transfer.

title: MY BODY IS A CAGE
shared_component: shared transformer policy without explicit morphology graph reliance
interface: set/sequence-style body representation for incompatible control
why_it_matters: Important cautionary evidence that explicit morphology graphs do not automatically improve shared control.

title: Humanoid Policy ~ Human Policy
why_it_matters: Directly relevant to human-to-humanoid cross-embodiment transfer.

title: Human-Humanoid Cross-Embodiment Behavior-Skill Transfer
why_it_matters: Early humanoid-specific cross-embodiment formulation.

title: HumanoidExo
why_it_matters: Reduces the human-humanoid gap at data collection time.

title: HumanX
why_it_matters: Shows human video as a scalable source for humanoid interaction skills.

title: ZeroWBC
why_it_matters: Connects egocentric human perception directly to humanoid whole-body control.

title: UniSkill
why_it_matters: A key paper on scalable cross-embodiment skill abstraction.

title: One Policy to Run Them All: Towards an End-to-end Learning Approach to Multi-Embodiment Locomotion
why_it_matters: The early URMA framing that directly targets one locomotion policy across embodiments.

title: H-Zero
why_it_matters: A core reference for cross-humanoid locomotion transfer.

title: XHugWBC
why_it_matters: Probably the closest paper to one-policy multi-humanoid locomotion.

title: HugWBC
why_it_matters: Strong baseline for unified whole-body control.

title: General Humanoid Whole-Body Control via Pretraining and Fast Adaptation
why_it_matters: Supports a shared-prior plus adapter thesis direction.

title: Any2Any
embodiments: multiple humanoid platforms including Unitree G1 LimX Oli and LimX Luna
embodiment_specific: kinematic alignment + lightweight PEFT dynamics adaptation
why_it_matters: Shows pretrained whole-body tracking specialists can transfer to new humanoids with only a small fraction of full training data and compute.

title: Behavior Foundation Model for Humanoid Robots
why_it_matters: Important for promptable shared policy design.

title: One Policy to Run Them All: an End-to-end Learning Approach to Multi-Embodiment Locomotion
evaluation: unseen embodiment transfer and real-world validation
why_it_matters: The main URMA paper and a core reference for shared locomotion policies across body plans.

title: Efficient Morphology-Aware Policy Transfer to New Embodiments
interface: morphology-conditioned policy with adapters or prefix tuning
why_it_matters: Directly relevant if your system uses shared pretraining plus lightweight embodiment-specific adaptation.

title: LocoFormer
embodiments: procedurally generated legged and wheeled robots; evaluated on unseen robots
interface: context-based locomotion adaptation without explicit kinematics
data_source: large-scale reinforcement learning on procedurally generated robots
evaluation: unseen robot adaptation + real-world zero-shot transfer
why_it_matters: A key in-context adaptation paper showing that long-context memory can support cross-embodiment locomotion transfer.

title: CEI
why_it_matters: One of the cleanest interface-centric papers for your topic.

title: Learning a Unified Latent Space for Cross-Embodiment Robot Control
embodiments: humans + single-arm dual-arm and legged humanoid robots
interface: latent control space learned with contrastive alignment
why_it_matters: A clean latent-control formulation for aligning human motion with diverse humanoid platforms.

title: Latent Action Diffusion
why_it_matters: Shows why aligning latent action can be easier than aligning raw actions.

title: One-Policy-Fits-All
why_it_matters: Directly relevant to one-policy design.

title: X-Sim
why_it_matters: Useful if object motion is a better shared variable than human joint motion.

title: LAP
why_it_matters: Opens a path to language-like action interfaces.

title: Contact-conditioned learning of locomotion policies
embodiments: biped robot with multiple gait and contact modes
evaluation: single policy across multiple gaits and transitions
why_it_matters: A useful reference for contact-centric interfaces in shared locomotion policies.

title: Cross-Hand Latent Representation for Vision-Language-Action Models
why_it_matters: Important if you want the repository to cover cross-embodiment VLA and dexterous manipulation.

title: EgoMimic
why_it_matters: Useful for scaling robot learning from egocentric human demonstrations.

title: Object-Centric Dexterous Manipulation from Human Motion Data
why_it_matters: Important for object-centric transfer in dexterous manipulation.

title: DexFormer: Cross-Embodied Dexterous Manipulation via History-Conditioned Transformer
why_it_matters: A strong recent shared-policy paper for cross-embodied dexterous manipulation.

title: CEDex: Cross-Embodiment Dexterous Grasp Generation at Scale from Human-like Contact Representations
why_it_matters: A key dexterous-hand paper that links human-like contact representations to cross-embodiment grasping.

title: UniMorphGrasp
embodiments: diverse robotic hands with heterogeneous kinematic structures
shared_component: unified human-like canonical hand pose representation
interface: morphology-aware diffusion with hand kinematic graphs
evaluation: zero-shot generalization to unseen hand structures
why_it_matters: A recent morphology-aware diffusion approach for cross-embodiment dexterous grasp generation.

title: RoboMIND
why_it_matters: Useful benchmark for evaluating multi-embodiment transfer.

title: Humanoid-X / UH-1
embodiments: human video to humanoid pose control
why_it_matters: Key dataset for human-prior humanoid learning.

title: PHUMA
why_it_matters: Important because it prioritizes physically plausible humanoid motion.

title: Humanoid Everyday
why_it_matters: Useful benchmark for realistic humanoid manipulation.

title: AnyBody
why_it_matters: Helps design stronger evaluation protocols.

title: Towards Embodiment Scaling Laws in Robot Locomotion
why_it_matters: Important for deciding whether to scale data or embodiment diversity.

title: Multi-Embodiment Locomotion at Scale with extreme Embodiment Randomization
embodiments: 50 legged robots and millions of morphological variations
interface: improved embodiment-aware architecture with extreme randomization
evaluation: zero-shot transfer to unseen real humanoid and quadruped robots
why_it_matters: A major URMA-line scaling paper showing how embodiment diversity and randomization can be pushed much further.

title: McARL: Morphology-Control-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Generalizable Quadrupedal Locomotion
why_it_matters: A useful morphology-conditioned transfer baseline outside humanoids.

title: Multi-Loco: Unifying Multi-Embodiment Legged Locomotion via Reinforcement Learning Augmented Diffusion
shared_component: shared diffusion prior + shared residual actor
interface: zero-padded and normalized observation/action space with diffusion action priors
evaluation: simulation + real-world multi-platform deployment on seen embodiments
why_it_matters: Important evidence that a shared diffusion prior plus shared actor can unify locomotion across multiple legged embodiments, though it is not a strict unseen-embodiment transfer paper.

title: UniLegs
why_it_matters: Helpful if you want to extend humanoid work toward broader bipeds.

title: GCNT: Graph-Based Transformer Policies for Morphology-Agnostic Reinforcement Learning
why_it_matters: A strong graph-transformer baseline for morphology-agnostic policy learning.

title: Body Transformer: Leveraging Robot Embodiment for Policy Learning
why_it_matters: A useful architecture paper showing how robot embodiment can be injected directly into transformer policies.

title: Articulated-Body Dynamics Network Improves Policy Learning for Diverse Robotic Systems
evaluation: generalization to dynamics shifts and sim-to-real
why_it_matters: A promising recent paper on dynamics-grounded structural priors for cross-robot policy learning.

title: Cross-embodied Co-design for Dexterous Hands
why_it_matters: Extends cross-embodiment thinking from policy transfer to joint morphology-control co-design.

title: NerveNet
why_it_matters: A classic historical precursor for morphology-aware graph policies.
